title: Mirthful Middle Eastern Semolina Gratin
description: A vegan, bouncy-qq semolina gratin with a crispy robata‑grill crust, sweet maple syrup, and fragrant spices, perfect for a whimsical brunch dessert.
instructions: Turn on the robata grill to medium‑high heat.
In a large bowl, combine semolina, cardamom, cinnamon, salt and lemon zest until well mixed.
Add the vegan shortening to the dry mixture and mix until crumbly and clumpy.
In a small saucepan, heat almond milk, maple syrup and orange blossom water until just simmering, then remove from heat.
Pour the warm liquid over the semolina mixture and stir until the grains are tender and the mixture is moist.
Grease a 9‑inch Dutch oven and press the mixture into an even layer.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated robata grill and sear for 2 minutes on each side to form a crust.
Return the Dutch oven to the oven and bake for 15 minutes until golden brown.
Remove from heat, sprinkle toasted pistachios and chopped apricots over the top, drizzle a little extra maple syrup, and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
